title: aconitum × paniculatum
intro: Aconitum × paniculatum, also known as Monk's Hood or Aconitum, is a hybrid plant species within the Aconitum genus, characterized by its tall, upright growth and hood-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aconitum × paniculatum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and stimulate the immune system, although its toxicity and potential side effects must be carefully considered. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and processed into tinctures, teas, or capsules, but due to its toxicity, it is often combined with other herbs to minimize risks.
